data.title: Fiera Capital's assets under management decrease to $164.1 billion as of Dec 31, 2025, from $166.9 billion as of Sep 30, 2025.
data.description: Montreal, January 29, 2026 - Fiera Capital Corporation (TSX: FSZ) announced that as of December 31, 2025, their assets under management (AUM) were estimated at around $164.1 billion, a decrease from $166.9 billion as of September 30, 2025. The breakdown by distribution channel showed Institutional AUM at $93.6 billion compared to $94.5 billion in September 2025 and $90.1 billion in December 2024. This represented a decrease of $0.9 billion or 0.9% quarter-over-quarter and an increase of $3.6 billion or 3.9% year-over-year. Financial Intermediaries had AUM of $56.7 billion (compared to $58.4 billion in September 2025 and $62.4 billion in December 2024), showing a decrease of $1.7 billion or 2.9% quarter-over-quarter and a decrease of $5.7 billion or 9.1% year-over-year. Private Wealth AUM stood at $13.7 billion (versus $14.0 billion in September 2025 and $14.6 billion in December 2024), indicating a decrease of $0.3 billion or 2.0% quarter-over-quarter and a decrease of $0.9 billion or 6.1% year-over-year. The total AUM of $164.1 billion decreased by $2.9 billion or 1.7% quarter-over-quarter and by $3.0 billion or 1.8% year-over-year. The average quarterly AUM was $166.4 billion, showing an increase of $2.8 billion or 1.7% quarter-over-quarter but a decrease of $0.2 billion or 0.1% year-over-year. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Serial entrepreneurs raise $3.9M to build AI system for electronics procurement, led by 100Unicorns.
data.description: An electronics procurement platform called 1Buy.AI, focusing on AI technology, based in Delhi, India, revealed a seed round of $3.9 million. This funding was spearheaded by 100Unicorns, a notable investor.

data.title: Docebo Inc. plans to repurchase up to US$60,000,000 of its outstanding common shares at a price of US$20.40 per share.
data.description: In Toronto, Docebo Inc. (NASDAQ:DCBO; TSX:DCBO), a prominent learning platform provider focusing on artificial intelligence and innovation, has revealed the approval of a significant issuer bid. The company plans to repurchase and cancel up to US$60,000,000 of its common shares at a rate of US$20.40 per share. This move was sanctioned by the board of directors to enhance shareholder value and strengthen the company's position in the market.
